marketState: neutral — extreme 8-16D weather demand bullish catalyst offset by bearish seasonality, storage overhang, and weak weekly trend
summary: Price at $3.275 sits slightly above the fair-value midpoint of $2.96 but well within the band ($2.46–$3.46), neither stretched nor cheap. The dominant near-term signal is exceptional weather: 8-16D demand is ABOVE the 5-year historical maximum (+58% vs 5y, rangePos 2.05, aboveMax true) while a +26.2 BCF/day 24-hour demand surge has pushed total demand to +36% above 5-year normal — an extreme heat-wave setup that is the clearest bullish catalyst in the data. The fair-value model is constructive: fair value rises from $2.96 today to $3.18 by day 21, meaning the current $0.315 premium is expected to normalize through fair value rising (not price falling), leaving current levels roughly justified on a 3-week view. Against this, seasonal headwinds are pronounced — only 19% probability of being higher in 4 weeks with a typical -1.8% 4-week move (low reliability, 51pp spread) — and storage at +151 Bcf above the 5-year average delivered a bearish injection surprise (+6 Bcf vs forecast). Managed money at the 17th short percentile (5-year) represents a crowded short position that could fuel a squeeze if the heat wave sustains. Futures curve data was unavailable due to data-feed failures.

longTerm.rationale: Seasonal headwinds strongly dominate the 3-12 week view: probability of being higher in 4 weeks is only 19% with a typical -1.8% 4-week move and low seasonal reliability (51pp 10-90 spread). Storage at +151 Bcf above the 5-year average with a bearish injection surprise adds persistent supply-side pressure. The weekly trend is bearish with a 50<200 EMA death cross, and the fair-value model converges to ~$3.18 by day 21 — supporting the case that price drifts flat-to-lower over the next month rather than extending. COT at the 17th percentile is a genuine contrarian long setup but needs a stronger directional trigger before committing for 3-12 weeks.
longTerm.invalidation: Storage surplus expands beyond +225 Bcf vs 5y on back-to-back bearish surprises, or price collapses through $2.85 (below fair-value band floor trajectory), signaling seasonal forces are dominant and the COT squeeze thesis has failed.
longTerm.trigger: Wait for price to retrace to the $3.00–$3.12 zone (consistent with fair-value midpoint and the typical -1.8% seasonal 4-week drift) OR for storage surplus to narrow below +75 Bcf vs 5y before entering a multi-week long targeting September seasonal peak (+3.1% historically strongest month).

shortTerm.sizing: 1R approx 1% risk; scale out 50% at TP1, trail stop on remainder
shortTerm.rationale: The 8-16D demand window is above the 5-year historical maximum (rangePos 2.05, aboveMax true, +58% vs 5y) — an extreme heat-wave signal — reinforced by a +26.2 BCF/day 24-hour surge bringing demand to +36% above 5-year normal at 170 BCF/day. Managed money at the 17th short percentile provides substantial short-squeeze fuel. The 4H technical frame is the most constructive across all timeframes (bullish trend, positive MACD histogram, 50 EMA > 200 EMA, RSI 53) and price has $0.19 of room to the fair-value band ceiling at $3.46 before becoming structurally stretched.
shortTerm.invalidation: A 4H close below $3.12 signals the weather premium is unwinding and fair-value gravity is reasserting toward the $2.96 midpoint; alternatively, a sharp downward revision in 8-16D weather forecasts removing the above-maximum demand signal would negate the thesis immediately.

Managed money net short at 17th 5-year percentile (net -84,909 contracts) — historically crowded short with short-squeeze potential
